column1: # Lead Research Ledger
## How to Use This Ledger
- Each row represents a potential lead (individual or account) researched from public sources.
- Information is strictly from public sources: company websites
- Do not add non-public information (personal emails not published
- Update the "Notes" field with the source and date of research.
- Status options: New
column9: N/A.

column1: ## Columns
- Date researched: ISO date when lead was researched
- Product: Which product this lead is relevant to (EvidenceVault
- Account: Company name
- Contact: Full name of the contact person
- Title: Job title of the contact
- Public source: Where the information was found (e.g.
- Contact info: Public contact information (e.g.
- Region: Geographic region (North America
- Pain hypothesis: Specific pain point this lead likely experiences based on public info
- Suggested product: Which product we think is the best fit
- Suggested angle: How we would approach this lead
- Priority score: 1-5 based on fit and urgency
- Status: Current status in the outreach process
- Next action: Concrete next step to take
- Notes: Source URLs
